title: verbascum × krokeense
intro: Verbascum × krokeense, also known as the Krokeense Mullein, is a hybrid plant species of the Verbascum genus, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers and coarse, woolly leaves. The leaves and flowers of the Krokeense Mullein plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of health issues, due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant has been used to relieve respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to treat skin irritations and wounds. Krokeense Mullein can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal salves and creams to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
